### 2026-03-18 — Commit 094847a

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (scraper URL filtering) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Added exclusion patterns for template/gallery pages, sitemap XML, event/webinar pages, and individual integration subpages to the scraper URL filter. |

|-------|-------|

This limits confidence but does not negate the presence of a named billable unit." Also cleared zoominfo cache. |

### 2026-03-18 — Commit c3ee099

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (citation/evidence cleanup) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Applied `cleanEvidenceLine()` sanitization to both `sourceEvidence` snippets from LLM output and evidence entries derived from `observed` arrays. Previously only raw LLM snippets were cleaned; observed-derived entries could contain malformed markdown fragments. |
| Inferred Reason | Broken markdown artifacts (unclosed brackets, escaped backslashes) were appearing in citation output, likely from observed entries that bypassed cleaning. |

### 2026-03-18 — Commit a5597b3

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (page selection priority) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Refactored compare/vs page capping logic. Instead of inline loop with counter, splits links into compare vs non-compare sets, reserves max 2 compare slots, then fills remaining slots with non-compare (evidence-rich) pages first. |
| Inferred Reason | The previous inline counter approach (bcbbc1b) was causing a regression where compare pages could still crowd out pricing/product pages depending on sort order. This fix guarantees non-compare pages get priority. |

---

### 2026-03-18 — Commit b20c714

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (evidence text sanitization) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Applied `sanitizeEvidenceText()` to HTML-extracted accordion/table content before it enters the pipeline. Previously, raw HTML push was only stripping tags but not cleaning markdown artifacts. |
| Inferred Reason | Accordion-extracted content (pricing FAQ, feature tables) was passing through malformed markdown into downstream scoring, causing citation artifacts in reports. |

---

### 2026-03-18 — Commit f7688da

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (evidence sanitization + structured pricing data) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Added `sanitizeEvidenceText()` and `sanitizeEvidenceList()` helper functions to strip markdown artifacts (images, malformed links, formatting chars) from all evidence text. Applied sanitization to: (1) full page markdown before return, (2) all structured JSON fields (valueUnit, trialDetails, refundPolicy, plan names/prices/features/limits). Also fixed Firecrawl v1 API integration by moving JSON extraction config to `jsonOptions` top-level param instead of inline in formats array. |
| Inferred Reason | Structured pricing data and page markdown were passing through raw markdown syntax (image refs, broken links) into scoring prompts, causing LLM to generate citation artifacts. The Firecrawl API call was also using an incorrect format for JSON extraction. |

---

### 2026-03-17 — Commit 51be133

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (citation cleanup regex) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Refined malformed-markdown cleanup regexes in `cleanEvidenceLine()`: changed `$` anchors to mid-line matching (`\n` boundary) and narrowed the catch-all unclosed bracket pattern to max 50 chars to avoid over-stripping. |
| Inferred Reason | Previous regex with `$` anchor (from 9c620b0) was too aggressive — it could strip legitimate content. Also wasn't catching malformed fragments mid-line, only at end of line. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit bcbbc1b

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (page selection priority — pricing vs compare pages) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Added compare/vs page cap: max 2 compare pages allowed in scrape selection. Previously, compare pages could consume all slots. Uses inline counter to skip compare pages after the 2nd one. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit 23d2138

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (URL scoring for page prioritization) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Added three new URL scoring boosts: (1) +800 for pricing/billing keywords anywhere in path (catches /faqs/pricing, /resources/pricing), (2) +200 for /features/ pages, (3) -200 penalty for /compare and /vs- pages. |
| Inferred Reason | Pricing pages nested under non-standard paths were not being prioritized. Compare pages were over-represented. This rebalances scrape slot allocation toward evidence-rich pages. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit 0ee83bd

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | guardrail |
| Dimension(s) Affected | Safety rails and trust surfaces (primarily), all dimensions |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Added two new evidence quality rules to the scoring prompt: (1) **LEGAL vs PRODUCT EVIDENCE** — privacy policies, biometric notices, and compliance language must NOT be cited as evidence for safety rails or admin controls; safety evidence must come from product/pricing/docs/trust pages. (2) **STRUCTURED DATA CAVEAT** — machine-extracted sections must not be cited as direct quotes. |
| Inferred Reason | The LLM was citing legal boilerplate (e.g., "monitor usage of our Service" from privacy policies) as evidence of safety rails, inflating Safety dimension scores. Also, AI-paraphrased structured data was being treated as direct quotes. |

---

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|

| Dimension(s) Affected | All (structured pricing data labeling) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Changed the structured pricing data section header from "Machine-Extracted" to "Machine-Extracted — NOT direct quotes" and added a warning note: "Do NOT cite these as direct quotes. Use only as supplementary context for scoring." |
| Inferred Reason | LLM was treating machine-extracted pricing JSON as verbatim quotes in citation output, creating accuracy issues in reports. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit efc5b30

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | scoring_logic + confidence_threshold |
| Dimension(s) Affected | Value unit definition |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Generalized the value-unit score floor logic. Previously only triggered on explicit credit FAQ signals (`hasExplicitCreditFaqSignals`); now also triggers when there are ≥2 evidence digest value-unit signals. Confidence floor is 0.65 for explicit FAQ signals, 0.55 for general value-unit signals. Changed from hardcoded rationale to preserving the LLM's original rationale with an appended note. Updated uncertainty reason to clarify metering granularity is a "1→2 gap" not a 0 condition. |
| Inferred Reason | Companies with named value units (but not explicit FAQ credit signals) were scoring 0 on this dimension because the floor only activated on a narrow condition. The LLM's rationale was also being replaced with a hardcoded string, losing context. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit b42bef7

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | scoring_logic + evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All dimensions (pricing page contradiction fix) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts`, `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Major pricing data extraction overhaul: (1) Added PRICING PAGE VERIFICATION instruction to the LLM prompt — injects the list of scraped URLs and forbids the LLM from claiming "no public pricing page" if a pricing URL was scraped. (2) Added post-LLM contradiction fix that regex-scans all dimension rationales, weaknesses, and recommendedFocus for "no pricing page" claims and rewrites them to reference the actual scraped pricing URL. Cleared beautiful.ai cache. |
| Inferred Reason | The LLM was hallucinating "no public pricing page" in reports even when a pricing page had been successfully scraped and its content was in the prompt. This caused incorrect weakness entries and inflated recommended focus items. Two-layer fix: prompt-level prevention + post-hoc regex correction. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit 650a76a

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | guardrail |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All dimensions |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Added post-LLM contradiction fix: after scoring, scans all dimension rationales, weaknesses, and recommendedFocus for patterns claiming "no public pricing page." If a pricing URL was actually scraped, rewrites the contradictory text to reference the real pricing page. Covers 6 regex patterns for different phrasings. |
| Inferred Reason | LLM was contradicting its own evidence — scraped pricing page content was in the prompt but the LLM still claimed no pricing page existed. This post-hoc guardrail catches and corrects the contradiction. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit 6c856ec

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | guardrail |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All dimensions |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Added PRICING PAGE VERIFICATION instruction block to the evidence digest section of the scoring prompt. Lists all scraped URLs and instructs the LLM: "If any of these URLs contain '/pricing', '/plans', or '/billing', then a public pricing page EXISTS. You MUST NOT claim 'no public pricing page'." |
| Inferred Reason | Prompt-level prevention of the "no pricing page" hallucination — the LLM needed explicit instruction with the URL list to avoid contradicting scraped evidence. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit a11e761

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (structured pricing extraction) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Fixed Firecrawl v1 response handling: added `llm_extraction` as an alternative field to `json` for structured pricing data extraction. Firecrawl v1 can return structured data in either `data.llm_extraction` or `data.json`. |
| Inferred Reason | Structured pricing extraction was silently failing for some pages because the code only checked `data.json` but Firecrawl was returning the data in `data.llm_extraction`. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit 36f7535

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (Firecrawl API integration fix) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Fixed Firecrawl v1 API call: moved JSON extraction config from inline format array object to top-level `jsonOptions` parameter. The formats array now only contains string values ('markdown', 'html', 'rawHtml', 'json'). Added `jsonOptions` with schema and extraction prompt as a separate request body field. |
| Inferred Reason | The Firecrawl v1 REST API expects `jsonOptions` as a top-level parameter, not embedded in the formats array. The previous approach (from 5d22b9d) was likely causing the structured extraction to fail silently. |

---

### 2026-03-17 — Commit 5d22b9d

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (new structured pricing extraction) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Major feature: Added structured JSON extraction for pricing pages via Firecrawl. Defines a `pricingJsonSchema` with fields for plans (name, price, billing period, features, limits, overage policy), valueUnit, free tier, trial, refund policy, enterprise pricing, cost drivers, and discounts. Pricing pages are detected via URL pattern and get an additional 'json' format request. Extracted JSON is appended to page markdown as a "Structured Pricing Data" section with plan details, cost drivers, and discounts formatted as markdown. |
| Inferred Reason | Raw markdown scraping of pricing pages was insufficient for accurate scoring — complex pricing tables, accordion content, and dynamic elements were being lost. Structured extraction provides deterministic data fields the scoring engine can rely on. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit 25362fb

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | guardrail + scoring_logic |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All 8 dimensions |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Multi-part change: (1) Added ANTI-HALLUCINATION rule to scoring prompt — rationale must only describe pricing constructs explicitly present in evidence; must not fabricate credits, tokens, usage-based pricing, rollovers. (2) Added REFUND/TERMS COMPLETENESS rule — cite ALL material conditions, not just the first one. (3) Refactored `applyDigestFloor()` to preserve the LLM's original rationale instead of replacing it with a hardcoded string. Removed hardcoded `floorRationale` parameter from all dimension floor calls (ICP, Buyer, Pools, Overages, Safety). (4) Same preservation applied to cost-driver floor logic. (5) Bumped version to `anti-hallucination-v1`. |
| Inferred Reason | The LLM was fabricating pricing constructs (e.g., claiming a company uses credits when it uses flat-rate pricing) and the hardcoded floor rationales were doubling down on these fabrications. By preserving the original rationale and adding anti-hallucination instructions, the output now reflects actual evidence instead of pre-written assumptions. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit 4b0d1de

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | guardrail |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All dimensions (safety net refactor) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Refactored `applyDigestFloor()` helper: removed `floorRationale` parameter entirely. Instead of replacing the LLM's rationale with a hardcoded string, the function now appends "[Score floored to 1 based on N public evidence signals.]" to the existing rationale. Added comment: "IMPORTANT: Preserves the AI's original rationale to avoid fabricating pricing constructs." |
| Inferred Reason | Core fix for the anti-hallucination effort. The hardcoded floor rationales were making specific claims about pricing constructs that didn't exist for many companies. Preserving the original rationale ensures the output reflects actual evidence. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit 6ac7dd2

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | guardrail |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All dimensions |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Added two critical output rules to the scoring prompt: (1) **ANTI-HALLUCINATION**: rationale must only describe pricing constructs explicitly present in scraped content — no fabricating credits, tokens, usage-based pricing, rollovers, or top-ups. If flat-rate seat-based pricing, say so. If not documented, say "not publicly documented." (2) **REFUND/TERMS COMPLETENESS**: cite ALL material conditions found (processing fees, pro-rata charges, trial conversion rules), not just the first one. |
| Inferred Reason | LLM was inventing pricing models (e.g., claiming credit-based pricing for flat-rate products) and only citing partial refund terms. These guardrails force evidence-faithful output. |

### 2026-03-17 — Commit daa8c86

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | evidence_pipeline |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (scraper architecture overhaul) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/scrape-website/index.ts` (350+ insertions, 441 deletions) |
| What Changed | Major refactor: replaced the previous link-discovery approach with a **map-first discovery** strategy. Now calls Firecrawl's `/map` API endpoint first to get a comprehensive sitemap of the domain (up to 200 URLs), then scores and selects the highest-value pages for scraping. Added `mapDomain()` helper, URL scoring function (`scoreUrl()`), exclusion patterns for low-value pages (legal, login, blog, careers, etc.), and high-intent path detection (pricing, security, trust, terms, etc.). |
| Inferred Reason | The previous link-following approach missed important pages (pricing, trust, terms) that weren't linked from the homepage. Map-first ensures comprehensive page discovery before selection, dramatically improving evidence coverage. |

---

### 2026-03-13 — Commit ce5fcc3

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | scoring_logic |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (re-run flow) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts`, `src/lib/api/scraper.ts`, `src/pages/Results.tsx` |
| What Changed | Implemented evidence re-run flow: (1) Parses request body early to detect re-runs (presence of `previousScores`). (2) Skips rate limiting for re-runs. (3) Reuses existing company profile instead of re-extracting it. (4) Skips scan_usage recording for re-runs. Added `existingProfile` to the `AnalyzeRequest` interface. |
| Inferred Reason | Users needed to re-run scoring with updated evidence (e.g., after insider answers) without hitting rate limits or burning LLM calls on redundant profile extraction. |

---

### 2026-03-13 — Commit 89931d9

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | gate_change |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (rate limiting gate) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Made scan_usage recording conditional: skips inserting usage records for evidence re-runs (`isRerun`). |
| Inferred Reason | Re-runs were double-counting against the user's scan quota, effectively penalizing users for refining their results. |

---

### 2026-03-13 — Commit 4ea10c5

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | gate_change |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (request parsing) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Removed duplicate `req.json()` parse that was left after the request body was moved to be parsed earlier (in f3d18c9). |
| Inferred Reason | Bug fix — the duplicate parse was causing the request body to be consumed twice, likely resulting in an empty body error on the second read. |

---

### 2026-03-13 — Commit f3d18c9

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | gate_change |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (rate limiting gate) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Moved request body parsing (`req.json()`) above the rate limit check. Detects re-runs by checking for `previousScores`. Skips rate limiting for re-runs (`!isAdmin && !isRerun`). Logs "Skipping rate limit for evidence re-run". |
| Inferred Reason | Re-runs needed to bypass rate limiting, but the re-run detection required parsing the request body, which happened after the rate limit check. Moving the parse earlier allows the gate to distinguish initial scans from re-runs. |

---

### 2026-03-13 — Commit 838246a

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Category | scoring_logic |
| Dimension(s) Affected | All (request interface) |
| Files Changed | `supabase/functions/analyze-company/index.ts` |
| What Changed | Added `existingProfile` to the destructured request body — now extracts `{ pages, url, insiderAnswers, previousScores, existingProfile }` from the request. |
| Inferred Reason | Preparation for the re-run flow — the client needed to pass the existing company profile to avoid redundant LLM extraction on re-runs. |
